items.question: What is bentonite
items.answer: Bentonite is a naturally occurring clay primarily composed of the mineral montmorillonite. It is known for its excellent swelling water absorption binding sealing and adsorption properties making it suitable for applications across drilling foundry iron ore pelletization civil engineering animal feed paper pharmaceuticals cosmetics and many other industries.

items.question: What are the main types of bentonite
items.answer: The two main natural types are Sodium Bentonite and Calcium Bentonite. Depending on the intended application bentonite may also be processed activated or modified to achieve specific performance characteristics required for industrial use.

items.question: What is the difference between Sodium Bentonite and Calcium Bentonite
items.answer: Sodium bentonite is known for its high swelling capacity superior water absorption and excellent viscosity making it ideal for drilling fluids civil engineering sealing and environmental applications. Calcium bentonite has lower swelling characteristics but offers good adsorption and binding properties making it suitable for applications such as animal feed agriculture absorbents and certain industrial processes. The appropriate type depends on the intended application and performance requirements.

items.question: What is Montmorillonite and why is it important
items.answer: Montmorillonite is the primary clay mineral found in bentonite and is responsible for most of its valuable properties including swelling water absorption binding sealing and cation exchange capacity (CEC). In general a higher montmorillonite content contributes to improved performance although the required level varies depending on the intended application and industry.

items.question: What is Activated Bentonite
items.answer: Activated bentonite is bentonite that has been chemically or thermally processed to enhance specific properties for particular applications. Acid-activated bentonite is commonly used in edible oil purification bleaching and chemical processing while soda activation improves the swelling and rheological properties of calcium bentonite for applications such as drilling fluids and civil engineering. The activation method depends on the desired end use.

items.question: What is Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) in bentonite
items.answer: Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) is a measure of bentonite's ability to attract hold and exchange positively charged ions. This property contributes to its adsorption performance binding efficiency and interaction with water and other materials. CEC is particularly important in applications such as agriculture animal feed environmental remediation and water treatment where ion exchange plays a significant role in product performance.

items.question: How should bentonite be stored
items.answer: Bentonite should be stored in a clean dry and well-ventilated area protected from moisture rain and contamination. Bags should be kept sealed until use and preferably stored on pallets away from direct contact with the floor. Proper storage helps preserve product quality flowability and performance throughout its shelf life.

items.question: What is Foundry Grade Bentonite
items.answer: Foundry Grade Bentonite is a high-performance binder used in green sand molding systems for the production of ferrous and non-ferrous metal castings. items.question: What is KORVANTO BENTO CIVIL used for
items.answer: KORVANTO BENTO CIVIL is a high-performance sodium bentonite specially processed for civil engineering and foundation construction. It is used to prepare bentonite slurry that stabilizes excavations minimizes filtration loss supports borehole walls and improves drilling efficiency in piling diaphragm wall tunneling and trenching projects.

items.question: What is the difference between C-33 and C-45
items.answer:
The primary difference is the Marsh Cone slurry viscosity which determines slurry performance for different construction conditions.
KORVANTO BENTO CIVIL C-33 (Minimum 33-second Marsh Cone viscosity) is designed for general civil engineering applications such as bored piling slurry trenching and standard foundation works.
KORVANTO BENTO CIVIL C-45 (Minimum 45-second Marsh Cone viscosity) provides higher viscosity lower filtration loss improved suspension and greater borehole stability for demanding applications such as diaphragm walls tunnel boring (TBM) horizontal directional drilling (HDD) microtunneling and difficult ground conditions.

items.question: What is KORVANTO BENTO SEAL used for
items.answer: KORVANTO BENTO SEAL is a high-swelling sodium bentonite designed for pond sealing and water retention applications. It forms a low-permeability barrier that helps reduce seepage and water loss in ponds reservoirs canals lagoons and other water containment structures.

items.question: What is the difference between SEAL-P and SEAL-G
items.answer:
KORVANTO BENTO SEAL is available in two grades for different installation methods.
SEAL-P – Pond Sealant Powder Grade is finely ground bentonite used where the material is mixed with soil or incorporated into the pond base before compaction. It provides excellent coverage and uniform sealing.
SEAL-G – Pond Sealant Granular Grade consists of granules that are easier to spread and are commonly used for blanket application broadcasting or localized seepage repair. It offers improved handling with reduced dust generation.
The choice depends on the construction method soil conditions and installation requirements.

items.question: What is the difference between hydrous kaolin calcined kaolin and metakaolin
items.answer:
Hydrous kaolin is the naturally processed form used in ceramics paper paints plastics and rubber.
Calcined kaolin is heat-treated to improve brightness opacity electrical properties and reinforcement characteristics.
Metakaolin is a highly reactive calcined kaolin primarily used as a supplementary cementitious material in concrete construction chemicals and specialty building materials.
